---
pagetitle: "Hello World - Generality Labs"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    theme: none
    include-in-header: ../../../_includes/head.html
    include-before-body:
      - ../../../_includes/post-before.html
      - _header.html
    include-after-body: ../../../_includes/post-after.html
    link-external-newwindow: true
    embed-resources: false
---

Hello, world. The prose on this page is Quarto markdown rendered through the shared site shell. The widget below is a real React component — written in JSX in `components/HelloWorld.jsx`, bundled by `make react` into the committed static module `assets/gl-react.js` — dropped into the prose with a shortcode, no OJS required:

```default
{{{< react HelloWorld start=3 >}}}
```

{{< react HelloWorld start=3 >}}

The same component can also be mounted from an OJS cell — the route to use when props should be driven by reactive values (sliders, loaded data):

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
glr = import("/assets/gl-react.js")
glr.mount(glr.HelloWorld, { start: 100 })
```

And an arrow-function stress test, written as **actual JSX in this markdown file** — a ```` ```jsx ```` fence compiled by esbuild at render time. The component is a named export defined as an arrow function, with arrow handlers and arrows inside `.map`, taking a real array prop (no strings, no shortcode syntax):

```jsx
<ArrowDemo
  items={["arrows", "as", "props"]}
  label={(w) => w.toUpperCase()}
  onPick={(w) => console.log("[ArrowDemo] picked:", w)}
/>
```

Everything a component-bearing post needs works from here: markdown prose, `inline code`, [links](https://generality.org), OJS cells, and React islands with state — via shortcode for static props, via `glr.mount` for reactive ones.
